MQT11.title: Thermal Cycling Test (MQT 11)
MQT11.summary: Thermal cycling between -40 °C and +85 °C. 200 cycles for qualification, 50 for prequalification. Ramp rate <= 100 °C/h with dwell times >= 10 min at the extremes. Isc (or, optionally, the maximum power current) flows through the cells whenever the module temperature is above +25 °C.
MQT11.pass_fail: Module passes if: (a) no major visual defects per MQT 01; (b) post-test Pmax retained >= 95% of pre-stress Pmax (Gate 2, delta <= 5%); (c) wet leakage and dry insulation resistance still meet MQT 03 / MQT 15 limits; (d) no breaks in the bypass diode circuit during the test.

MQT12.title: Humidity Freeze Test (MQT 12)
MQT12.summary: 10 cycles alternating between +85 °C / 85 %RH (>= 20 h) and -40 °C (>= 0.5 h), with controlled transitions. Tests moisture ingress robustness through freeze stress on absorbed water.
MQT12.pass_fail: Module passes if Pmax retention >= 95% (Gate 2), no major visual defects, and wet leakage / insulation resistance limits per MQT 15 / MQT 03 are met after recovery.

MQT13.title: Damp Heat Test (MQT 13)
MQT13.summary: Continuous 1000 h soak at +85 °C / 85 %RH. Targets long-term moisture-driven degradation: encapsulant adhesion loss, corrosion, ionic drift.
MQT13.pass_fail: Pmax retention >= 95% after recovery (Gate 2). No major visual defects per MQT 01. Wet leakage current must still meet MQT 15 limit. Insulation resistance per MQT 03.

MQT18.title: Bypass Diode Thermal Test (MQT 18)
MQT18.summary: Module heated to +75 °C; each bypass diode forced to conduct at 1.0 x Isc for 1 h, then at 1.25 x Isc for one further hour. Diode junction temperature Tj is calculated from the forward-voltage drop using the diode datasheet's Vf(Tj) characteristic (or the -2 mV/°C silicon slope when only Vf at one Tj is known): Tj = Tj_ref + (Vf_ref - Vf_measured) / |dVf/dTj|. Tj must be obtained while current is flowing.
MQT18.pass_fail: Diode passes if Tj at the end of the stress remains below the manufacturer's maximum-rated Tj (typically <= 128 °C for standard Si Schottky/PN diodes per IEC 62979). The diode must remain functional (forward voltage stable, no open/short) and the module must still meet the safety requirements of IEC 61730.

MQT21.title: Light & elevated Temperature Induced Degradation pre-conditioning (MQT 21 — references IEC TS 63342)
MQT21.summary: Stabilises the module against LeTID using the dark-current-injection variant: inject Idark = Isc - Imp at 75 °C +/- 5 °C for >= 162 h. Measure Pmax at STC every 24 h (minimum every 54 h) to trace the degradation/recovery curve.
MQT21.pass_fail: Used as a pre-conditioning step rather than a hard pass/fail. Performance assessment per IEC TS 63342: total relative Pmax loss <= 2% from the pre-stress reference value, with the degradation curve having flattened (delta within 0.5%/24 h on the final measurement interval).

TS63342.title: LeTID Susceptibility Test for crystalline silicon modules
TS63342.summary: Standalone susceptibility procedure. Recommends dark current injection at Idark = Isc - Imp at module temperature 75 °C +/- 5 °C for at least 162 h, with intermediate STC IV sweeps. Reports the maximum observed Pmax loss (Gate value) and the time to peak degradation.
TS63342.pass_fail: Module is reported as compliant if maximum Pmax loss vs. pre-stress reference is <= 2% and the recovery branch is observed (Pmax stops decreasing within the test duration).

MST13.title: Ground Continuity Test (MST 13)
MST13.summary: Verifies the bonding path between every accessible conductive part and the module's grounding terminal. Force a current equal to 2.5 x the rated overcurrent protection (or 25 A, whichever is lower) for at least 2 minutes; measure the voltage drop across the path.
MST13.pass_fail: PASS if the calculated resistance R = V/I is < 0.1 Ohm AND the measured voltage drop across the bond is <= 2.5 V at the test current.

MST26.title: Reverse Current Overload Test (MST 26)
MST26.summary: Module subjected to a reverse current equal to 135% of the rated overcurrent protective-device current for 2 hours, with the module placed on cheesecloth/tissue to detect ignition.
MST26.pass_fail: PASS if no flame, no sustained arcing, no rupture of the module, and the tissue is not charred or ignited. The module is not required to remain electrically functional afterwards.
